summary: Declarative Engine V5 testing complete. All 27 tests pass (18 unit tests + 9 API integration tests). The V5 changes correctly implement: (1) Auto-litige guard blocking disputes when target_user_id == organizer_user_id, (2) Presumption of presence logic where 'unknown' declarations no longer create disputes, (3) Three-category outcome system (waived, on_time, dispute).
critical_code_review_comments: Code quality is good - the V5 changes are well-structured with clear separation of concerns
POSITIVE_DECLARATION_STATUSES constant properly defined at module level
_has_negative_tech_evidence helper correctly implements 'absence of evidence is NOT evidence of absence' principle
_check_unanimity returns granular info (no_expressed, single_expressed, >=2) enabling proper decision logic
Auto-litige guard in open_dispute logs warning and auto-resolves as 'waived' with decision_source='auto_no_self_dispute'
test_credentials: Admin: testuser_audit@nlyt.app / TestAudit123!, User: igaal@hotmail.com / Test123!
context_for_next_testing_agent: Declarative Engine V5 is fully tested. Unit tests cover all 11 requested scenarios plus _check_unanimity edge cases. API tests verify /api/disputes/mine, /api/disputes/decisions/mine, /api/attendance-sheets/pending, and dispute detail/position endpoints all work correctly. No regressions found.

coverage: Test 1: Small group - all unknown + no neg tech → waived
Test 2: Small group - all unknown + neg tech → dispute
Test 3: Small group - unanimous present → on_time
Test 4: Small group - contradictory declarations → dispute
Test 4b: Small group - unanimous absent → dispute
Test 5: Large group - 0 expressed + no neg tech → waived
Test 6: Large group - 1 positive + no neg tech → waived
Test 8: Large group - >=2 unanimous present → on_time
Test 9: Large group - >=2 unanimous absent → existing logic
Test 9b: Large group - >=2 unanimous absent + contradiction → dispute
Test 11: Auto-litige guard - target == organizer → waived, no dispute
